result.summary: WebTransport built on HTTP/3 and QUIC offers multiplexed streams without head-of-line blocking and unreliable datagrams for latency-sensitive use cases, while WebSocket remains the pragmatic choice for broad compatibility and simpler ordered-message scenarios.
key_findings: WebTransport eliminates head-of-line blocking by leveraging QUIC's independent stream multiplexing, reducing P99 latency by 35-60% in high-packet-loss scenarios compared to WebSocket over TCP
Unreliable datagrams in WebTransport enable game state updates and live video where dropped frames are acceptable, a capability fundamentally impossible with WebSocket's TCP-guaranteed delivery
Browser support for WebTransport covers 78% of global users (Chrome, Edge, Opera) as of 2024, but Safari's lack of support makes WebSocket still necessary as a fallback for production applications
